board.name: North Carolina Board of Nursing: Nurse Aide

hero.title: Becoming a CNA in North Carolina
hero.dek: Start with the training, testing, and registry requirements.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in North Carolina
seo.description: North Carolina CNA requirements, training, testing, and registry steps.
target_queries: how to become a cna in north carolina

faq.q: What training is required to become a CNA in North Carolina?
Is an exam required to become a CNA in North Carolina?
faq.a: Yes, a competency evaluation is required.

hero.title: North Carolina CNA State Application
hero.dek: Use the state's standard route if you are applying directly in North Carolina.

seo.title: North Carolina CNA State Application
seo.description: Apply for CNA certification in North Carolina through the standard state process.

cards.label: Timeline
cards.value: Varies by training and test completion

faq.q: What is the standard CNA application path in North Carolina?
faq.a: Complete approved training, pass the competency evaluation, then complete registry listing requirements.

hero.title: Transfer a CNA License to North Carolina
hero.dek: North Carolina may allow registry listing by reciprocity if you meet the out-of-state criteria.

seo.title: North Carolina CNA Reciprocity and Transfer
seo.description: Transfer a CNA license to North Carolina, including fee, timing, and eligibility.
target_queries: transfer cna license to north carolina

cards.label: Extra Steps
cards.value: Registry check, ID match, work-history review

faq.q: Is there a fee to transfer a CNA license to North Carolina?
faq.a: No, there is currently no fee.
